This black granite antelope sculpture was erected in 2000 by artist Lorenzo Pace. It is located in Lower Manhattan, Foley Square. We appreciated this work as a tribute to freedom, against the oppression of human beings. In front of the Supreme Court, it is doing all it can.

We saw this black modern sculpture from 2000 from distance and I thought it looks like damaged hammer. Coming near I saw that it wants to be some kind of fountain. I would never connect it with a hint to the slave trade's Middle Passage which is what apparently the artist Lorenzo Pace aimed to do.
